subtasks.description: Evaluate the bulk PII export request against data governance and privacy policies. Determine if the export is compliant with data protection regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.), access control policies, and organizational PII handling standards. Assess whether the analyst has appropriate authorization for bulk customer PII access.

plan.cost_rationale: This request requires policy evaluation BEFORE any data extraction occurs. The analyst is requesting bulk export of sensitive PII (names, emails, phone numbers), which triggers compliance, access control, and governance checks. This is not a straightforward data retrieval—it's a governance decision that must be validated against a policy registry (data protection regulations, organizational PII handling policies, and role-based access controls). A single policy_action subtask (mid tier) is sufficient to evaluate the multi-policy requirements (GDPR, CCPA, organizational policy, role authorization). No sql_gen or extract subtasks are included because the policy check must approve the request first; if approved, a separate follow-up request would be needed to execute the actual export. This prevents unauthorized PII disclosure and ensures compliance evaluation precedes data access.

output.reason: Bulk PII export by a data-analyst lacks required Legal privacy approval and CISO sign-off (POL-501), and violates data minimization since names, emails, and phone numbers are sensitive PII with no documented business-critical justification (POL-502). Audit logging/retention controls (POL-503) can be applied but do not authorize the export. Action denied pending required approvals.
applied_constraints: Provide business-critical justification or reduce scope to non-sensitive attributes
Enable audit logging with 30-day retention if approved
